Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Donald Mahnke, Esq., Quill & Arrow LLP, 310-933-4271 dmahnke@quillarrowlaw.com for Plaintiff.
THEODORE LEE, Esq., SHOOK, HARDY & BACON L.L.P. (415) 544-1900 tlee@shb.com Attorney for Defendant LUCID GROUP USA, INC.
Raquel Sharp, CSR# 10619 925-226-8222 raquel@transcriptlegal.com, Court Reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LUCID GROUP USA INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Granted. The court exercised its discretion to consider the untimely opposition papers. Assuming that the plaintiff asserts that the delegation clause is itself unconscionable, plaintiff fails to show any procedural unconscionability as to the delegation clause due to the opt out provision which he chose not to exercise.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
